input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page respects the system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Continue"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#0078D4, Dark=#4CC2FF}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The markup extension `{AppThemeBinding Light=…, Dark=…}` automatically reacts to the OS theme change at runtime — no event wiring needed.

---

## C# equivalent — `SetAppTheme`

```csharp
public partial class MainPage : ContentPage
{
    public MainPage()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Extension method on BindableObject
        this.SetAppTheme(BackgroundColorProperty, Colors.White, Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E"));

        var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 24 };
        label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);

        var button = new Button { Text = "Continue" };
        button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
            Color.FromArgb("#0078D4"), Color.FromArgb("#4CC2FF"));
        button.SetAppTheme(Button.TextColorProperty, Colors.White, Colors.Black);
    }
}
```

`SetAppTheme(BindableProperty, lightValue, darkValue)` does the same thing as the XAML markup extension but from code-behind.

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Automatic switching | Both approaches listen to `Application.RequestedThemeChanged` internally |
| Default value | If `Unspecified`, the `Light` value is used |
| Force a theme | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to override system preference |
| Resource dictionaries | For larger apps, define colors in `AppTheme`-keyed resource dictionaries and reference them with `{DynamicResource}` |
